evidence.statement: The Lua sources define weak/strong selection, upgrades, caps, and the semantic call sites of random_int and random_bool.

The exact inventoried spawner.lua defines 16 difficulty/sector parameter sets for weak selection, upgrade selection, simultaneous living-upgrade caps, and conditional boss promotion; Mission:CreateSpawner instantiates the current difficulty/sector entry with optional mission-specific data overrides for Spawner:NextPawn.
The native RNG implementation, state, and complete consumption order have not been traced for this build.

known_gaps: Rust does not parse or apply SectorSpawners, reproduce Spawner:NextPawn, or materialize the unknown identity at an unblocked spawn marker; it projects marker persistence and blocking only.
Native GetDifficulty, GetSector, GAME:GetSpawnList, random_int/random_bool behavior, RNG state, and complete pre-spawn call order are unresolved.

The parameter matrix does not itself determine spawn count, timing, locations, or roster; selection fallbacks, living caps, streak rules, and conditional promotion can change the nominal weak, upgrade, and boss outcomes.

evidence.statement: The exact inventoried Lua source assigns Mission_Tides to Env_Tides and defines deterministic Index-based lane progression, permanent next-lane spawn blocking, building-aware warnings, and current-lane water conversion.

Rust consumes live warning metadata, defers Mission_Tides danger until after queued attacks, applies grounded lethality and the observed one damage to flyers, destroys marked pods, and advances the serialized warning lane; focused tests include live-derived timing and flyer regressions.

known_gaps: Rust does not currently convert a flooded tile to Terrain::Water; it models marked unit, building, and pod consequences while leaving terrain unchanged.
Rust does not independently reproduce Env_Tides:Start or Env_Tides:Plan permanent Board:BlockSpawn calls; it consumes current spawn and warning state from the bridge.
Warning advancement shifts serialized danger bits instead of reconstructing Lua Index, building-shadow, and convert logic.
Queued-attacks-before-wave and one damage to flyers are live-derived runtime observations, not explicit Lua semantics or an independently traced native scheduler and SpaceDamage proof.
Shield, frozen, building, terrain, and native SpaceDamage.iTerrain edge conformance remains non-exhaustive.
This record excludes the NewTerrain=TERRAIN_SAND Terratide branches, presentation effects, and campaign environment selection.

evidence.statement: The exact inventoried Lua sources define Rocket Artillery's inherited cardinal target area with minimum range two, base damage 2, smoke behind the shooter, forward push, two damage-3 variants, and combined damage 4 variant.

Rust has distinct IDs and definitions for all four variants, family-specific smoke and killed-target push, and generic artillery targeting and invalid-replay handling exercised by Rocket-focused tests.

known_gaps: Native AddArtillery, SpaceDamage, push, smoke, and effect-order helper semantics have not been independently traced exhaustively for this build.
The B and AB variants have exact definition/mapping coverage but no dedicated end-to-end simulator case; the A variant supplies the upgraded live-derived regression.

Lua LineArtillery with OnlyEmpty=false permits intact building centers, while Rust deliberately filters ordinary artillery centers that would damage intact buildings; minimum range, blocker continuation, and board-edge extent also lack a Rocket-specific conformance test.
Exact conformance across every terrain, status, objective, and collision interaction remains unverified.

evidence.statement: The exact inventoried Advanced Edition Lua source defines Reverse Thrusters as a cardinal charge whose single backblast tile takes distance-scaled damage and smoke while the origin takes one self-damage; A and B extend range to three, and AB extends it to four.

Rust has distinct IDs and DashAway definitions for all four variants, implements landing legality, backblast damage and smoke, recoil, movement, scoring, and replay, and exercises the base family behavior in focused tests.

known_gaps: Native Board:IsBlocked, Pawn:GetPathProf, SpaceDamage, AddCharge, terrain traversal, and effect-order semantics have not been independently traced for this build.

All behavior-level simulator, solver, and replay tests use the base BruteKickBack ID; A, B, and AB have exact definition and mapping coverage but no exact-ID end-to-end case for range-three or range-four targeting and backblast damage.

No focused family test exhaustively pins landing legality across occupied units, buildings, mountains, and grounded-versus-flying water or chasm cases, intermediate obstacle traversal, and off-board backblast behavior.
Boost and Nanofilter timing are live-derived Rust regressions rather than facts established by this Lua body; exhaustive status, objective, terrain, and collision conformance remains unverified.

evidence.statement: The exact inventoried Lua source defines Aerial Bombs as a cardinal leap of two tiles, extended to three by the range upgrade, that damages and smokes each strict transit tile; its A, B, and AB variants raise damage, range, or both.

Rust has distinct IDs and definitions for all four variants, applies the family-specific transit effects and landing restrictions, and covers definitions, transit behavior, target enumeration, illegal landings, scoring, and replay in focused tests.

known_gaps: Native Board:IsBlocked, Pawn:GetPathProf, AddLeap, SpaceDamage, and effect-order semantics have not been independently traced for this build.
The A and AB damage-two variants have exact definition coverage but no exact-ID end-to-end simulator case.
Existing range-upgraded transit tests call sim_leap directly with the unchanged base ID and definition at distance three, bypassing B/AB dispatch and range-three target enumeration.
Exact conformance across every landing terrain, carried status, transit occupant, and effect-order interaction remains unverified.

evidence.statement: global.lua defines default GetTargetArea, GetTargetScore, ScoreList, and ScorePositioning logic for this observed content revision.
Remaining disagreement is expected to include native candidate generation, movement/path enumeration, and tie-breaking.

known_gaps: The Rust next-turn enemy model is heuristic rather than a direct implementation of the Lua scoring tournament.
Candidate evaluation order and native tie-breaking are not captured.

evidence.statement: Enemy weapon effects and many custom target-score overrides are readable in the base and Advanced Edition Lua sources.
Rust maps known enemy weapon identifiers to WId values and resolves queued attacks in simulate_enemy_attacks.

known_gaps: Coverage is broad but not generated or audited one-to-one against every shipped GetSkillEffect and target override.
Native effect helpers can retain behavior not explicit in Lua.

evidence.statement: Player weapon target areas and SkillEffects are defined across shipped weapon Lua files.
Rust WId definitions and simulator cases independently implement many player weapon families.

known_gaps: The generated lexical ID index maps exact Lua constructor candidates to wid_from_str, but it does not tie every weapon function to Rust simulator cases and focused tests.
Two-click target legality and native helper semantics remain especially important audit targets.

evidence.statement: The exact inventoried Lua source defines Titan Fist base damage 2 and push, Dash as an unlimited directional charge, Damage as 4, and the combined AB variant.

Rust has distinct IDs and definitions for all four Titan Fist variants, dispatches base/B as melee and A/AB as charge, and exercises targeting, impact, terrain, upgrade, and bridge-replay behavior in focused tests.

known_gaps: Native GetProjectileEnd, Board:GetSimplePath, AddCharge, and AddMelee helper semantics have not been independently traced for this build.
The damage-only B variant has a definition test but no dedicated end-to-end simulator test; AB supplies the focused upgraded-damage charge evidence.
Exact conformance across every obstacle, edge, and status interaction remains unverified.

evidence.statement: Mission base classes and callbacks define objectives, turn limits, scripted pawns, and mission-specific behavior.
The repository extracts static mission metadata and implements selected mission transitions in the Rust simulator.

known_gaps: Dynamic Lua callbacks cannot all be recovered by the static metadata extractor.
Mission coverage is not yet audited against every base and Advanced Edition mission script.

evidence.statement: Environment selection and effect callbacks are readable in environments.lua and mission-specific scripts.
Rust independently applies environment danger and selected mission-specific ordering, including Mission_Tides.

known_gaps: The index does not yet enumerate every environment subclass and callback.
Native turn orchestration remains authoritative where Lua callback order is ambiguous.

evidence.statement: The exact inventoried Advanced Edition Lua source assigns Mission_Wind to Env_RandomWind; MarkBoard exposes two selected lanes, Plan chooses WindDir and non-repeating indices with random_bool/random_int, and ApplyEffect emits push-only SpaceDamage in direction-dependent scan order.

Rust routes live wind warnings out of damage handling, converts engine WindDir into solver coordinates, and pushes initially occupied marked tiles before queued attacks; four focused tests, including three live-derived regressions, cover marker classification, blocked-push grid damage, pre-gust corpse cleanup, and raw-direction conversion.

known_gaps: Rust does not reproduce Env_RandomWind:Plan RNG, LastIndices exclusion, or native RNG state/call order; it consumes the current bridge-supplied lane mask and direction.
Exact native environment-versus-attack orchestration is not independently traced for this build; pre-attack ordering is supported by live regressions rather than a native scheduler proof.
Bridge marked-lane/WindDir extraction and class fallback remain runtime dependencies and are not exhaustively conformance-tested across every mission state.
This record covers Mission_Wind with RandomWind=true and Freeze=false, not generic, nonrandom, or freeze subclasses, presentation effects, or campaign environment selection.

evidence.statement: The exact inventoried Final Cave Lua source alternates rock and tentacle modes across a four-phase selector, removes a selected BigBomb tile, marks selected tiles dangerous, and emits deadly rock-to-road or tentacle-to-lava effects.

For Mission_Final_Cave, Rust consumes bridge-supplied danger tiles, overrides stale flying-immunity metadata, and applies marked lethal danger to flyers as well as grounded units; a focused live-derived regression proves that boundary.

known_gaps: Rust consumes bridge-supplied danger tiles and does not reproduce Env_Final:Start, the four-phase selector, mode alternation, RNG, quarter or lava-cluster selection, crossing-path planning, Instant or Ordered scheduling, water targeting, BigBomb exclusion, or Board:SetDangerous enemy avoidance.
Rust does not implement Env_Final terrain mutations to TERRAIN_ROAD or TERRAIN_LAVA, mode identity, crack, shake, dropper, animation, or sound effects.

The focused proof covers one flying mech and stale-v2 correction, not grounded units, buildings, pods, shield or frozen behavior, terrain aftermath, selection, exact timing, legacy-v1 behavior, or exhaustive bridge extraction.
Native Env_Attack, GetQuarters, GetCrossingPath, RNG, SpaceDamage, and scheduler semantics have not been independently traced for this build.
This record covers Env_Final in Mission_Final_Cave and does not cover Env_Volcano or the preceding Mission_Final stage.

evidence.statement: The exact inventoried Advanced Edition Lua source defines Control Shot as an adjacent cardinal first click gated by IsControllable, followed by native path-profile movement within maximum budgets of 2, 3, 3, or 4 tiles; aside from TipImage metadata, A, B, and AB change only MoveDistance.
Rust has all four exact IDs, mappings, and definitions plus bespoke target enumeration, reachability, simulation, landing effects, achievement events, and 11 focused tests.
Rust's enemy-only eligibility predicate differs materially from the Lua IsControllable body, and native/UI behavior for predicate cases not represented by Rust has not been established.

known_gaps: Rust adds enemy-only and non-extra-tile requirements absent from Lua, omits Lua's powered, guarding with burrower exception, and nonzero base-move predicates, rejects a zero-current-speed grappled pawn that Lua may admit, and does not preserve Lua's explicit Snowmine1 or VIP_Truck exceptions.
The source predicate mismatch does not by itself prove every otherwise qualifying ally is live-controllable because native IsPowered and related pawn methods remain untraced.

Native Board:GetReachable, Board:GetPath, Pawn:GetPathProf, IsPowered, IsGuarding, IsGrappled, GetMoveSpeed, GetBaseMove, AddMove, AddLeap, and AddBurrow semantics have not been independently traced for this build.
A and B have exact definition and mapping coverage but no exact-ID behavior test; base and AB supply the current behavior evidence.

Destination reachability, path profile, terrain, status, and landing-effect equivalence is not exhaustive; Rust uses independent reachability and direct coordinate mutation rather than native path-effect sequencing.
The projectile-blocker condition has no corresponding explicit Lua check and is normally redundant at fixed Range=1; current blocker tests use a distance-two target that is already out of range.
There is no focused replay or exact bridge/native UI conformance proof, and raw bridge execution has not supplied reliable Let's Walk achievement credit.
